question.text: Section 12 of the PMLA 2002 requires reporting entities to maintain KYC records for how long after a business relationship with a client has ended?

answer.text: 3 years after the business relationship ends
7 years after the business relationship ends
10 years after the business relationship ends
5 years after the business relationship ends

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 12 of PMLA 2002 obligates reporting entities to maintain KYC records for 5 years after the business relationship with the client has ended. This is distinct from the 5-year transaction record retention (which runs from the transaction date). **The Trap:** 10 years sounds authoritative and candidates often confuse the KYC retention period with other regulatory timelines. The key distinction is the trigger — it starts from when the business relationship ends, not from the transaction date. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A and Series X-B also test KYC record maintenance periods under PMLA. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even after a client relationship ends, maintain KYC records for at least 5 years. Premature destruction of records can attract penalties under PMLA.
